violations.text: A sponge was observed in the hand-washing sink in the meat area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
The hand-washing sink in the meat area is lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

Sausage was observed held at 102 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025

The door to the outside, located in the walk-in cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per interview with PIC, service has been contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
Drinks and meats were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler/freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/02/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. Per interview with PIC, part has been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving, fans, gaskets have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

Recently sliced cheese was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety registered with RIDOH/CFP. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. Staff set-up sanitizer in 3-bay during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Chicken was observed held between 115- and 120- degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Cheese, ham, and sliced tomatoes in prep unit were observed held at 49-50, degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Chicharones and beef prepared 2/17/23 were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, portioned chicken and beef prepared in the morning were placed warm in the prep unit to cool. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
The area surrounding the dumpster had debris and litter. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024

The walls in the basement area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. A hole was observed near the walk-in refrigerator, black residue was observed on rear wall. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2024
